name: Legio
description: The Legio is a light escort carrier, designed for the Navarchs' smaller fleets. Like its bigger sibling, the Regnum, it features Phase Torpedoes, allowing it to provide fire support from afar while its assigned fighter complement tangles up the enemy.

name: Regnum
description: The Navarchs specialize in sea-to-space ship designs, and the mighty Regnum is a premiere feature of that ideology. A massive carrier, modest in armament, but encased in ultra-heavy shields with a heavy duty hull. With its payload of 46 fighters on tap, it's an impressive ship by all measures.

name: Regio
description: The mighty Regio-class carrier is a sight to behold, either on the seas or among the stars. While it does show its age with a lack of Phase Torpedo tubes, refits over generations mean many of these ships use state-of-the-art shield and hull technologies, resulting in a ship that not only has a payload of nearly 100 fighters, but also is practically unkillable in the face of all but the largest of co-ordinated strikes.

name: Epiregalimen
description: The Epiregalimen started life as an attempt to update the design of the Regio-class carrier. Though the old design still served its role well, the hope was to "modernize" it, providing a more flexible platform from which to launch fighters. Perhaps ironically, the design's original intent was almost completely lost; the new carrier's lack of phase torpedoes, in particular, render it an antiquated-on-arrival design. Not that the Navarchs couldn't find a use for it, of course: It now serves comfortably as a heavy-hitter carrier in the largest Navarch fleets, delivering its nearly two-hundred fighters where needed with aplomb.

name: Parvregalimen
description: The Parvregalimen is a hybrid freighter-carrier, designed as a solo mining vessel in contrast to the cludgey nature of supplementing Regnum-class carriers with Imperiums. Though its payload isn't quite as large as a Regnum, the Parvregalimen has a very large amount of cargo space, allowing it to be a reliable drop-off point for mining fighters.

name: Supravitae
description: To command a Supravitae is the highest honor a Navarch can achieve; and serving aboard one isn't very far behind. The Supravitae has a more formidable armament than any other Navarch ship, and support for a whopping 76 fighters, putting it between the Regnum and Regio in terms of fighter payload. Also aboard are some more logistical facilities, such as a notably sized cargo bay, and a large amount of spare bunks for reserve crew or ground troops. 	Of course, the Supravitae is not an easy ship to build or obtain; purchasing one will almost certainly leave a significant dent in any captain's budget for a long time.

name: Infrafamilia
description: An "Outrigger" refers both to a secondary hull attached to a ship as well as the ship itself capable of accepting such attachments. The smallest of the Navarchs' outrigger vessels, the Infrafamilia by itself is the smallest, lightest ship in the Navarch fleet, effectively mimicking the role of a Shuttle. Despite this, it is still a full-sized warship, with a decent armament allowing it to tangle with other small vessels and fighters. 	Outriggers are an aging technology in Navarch space, largely held afloat by pre-existing supply and infrastructure. Though most planetary settlements lack the correct equipment to properly interchange outriggers; instead, loaded variants of the ship must be found aboard space stations, where the interchange infrastructure is much simpler and easier to run.

name: Subsectio
description: A larger Outrigger, dating back to the Navarchs' transition from versatile, modular outriggers to dedicated ship designs about 35,000 years ago. The design has seen some refits to keep up with the latest technology, but is only configurable at larger Navarch stations due to the unwieldly length of the ship mixed with its three outrigger attachment points. 	By itself, however, the Subsectio has its own merits, essentially being a lighter armed but more durable and nimble Ordo. As such, some Navarchs use the "empty" configuration as a light screening destroyer, at least until they can reliably afford the slightly more expensive (and far less durable) Ordo.

name: Subforma
description: The Subforma is a stealth combat drone developed by the Navarchs; effectively just a standardized cargo container converted into a compact, stackable drone, Subformae rely on their carrier to recharge their shields, insteady relying on their dense hull and newly-developed rearmoring tech to keep the drone functional. Subformae carry anti-capital weapons, allowing a swarm to quickly rip a large target apart should their carrier's on-board armament be insufficient.

name: Forma
description: Building on techniques learned during the construction of the Subvarietas, the Forma-class fighter is designed as a deployable anti-missile net, allowing any fleet to rapidly extend its field of missile denial. The design does have some drawbacks, however, such as the reduced engine capacity and hampered outfit space, meaning the ship is only used in its dedicated anti-missile role.

name: Subvarietas
description: With their fighters being deployed in such significant numbers with the expectation of casualties, the Navarchs invested heavily in advanced automaton designs. The resultant fighters, known as the Subvarietas, are practically miniature warships, able to soak up an absurd amount of damage while doling out both anti-capital and anti-fighter fire. 	It's actually quite impressive how highly engineered these ships are; armor is strategically placed around every outfit compartment, reducing both the space and mass the armor takes up while providing maximum protection.

name: Tribus
description: The Tribus is a minelaying destroyer used by the Navarchs to saturate a system in lathe mines; though one Tribus is already capable of outputting a significant quantity of mines, they Tribus' relatively small size and low cost means they are frequently fielded in sorties of 3 or more.

name: Subfamilia
description: Compared to its sister ship, the Subfamilia is specialized for long-ranged missile combat rather than minelaying. In addition to 10 launch bays for Ransacker Missile swarms, the ship features a Monolithic Anti-Missile as one of its "main guns", allowing it to sweep mines and block missiles from enormous distances as it bombards the opposition from long range.

name: Genus
description: With its heavy armament of Phase Torpedoes, the Genus is a fearsome ranged combatant capable of doing highly accurate damage over great range. Its role as a dreaded capital hunter has lead to a popular folktale about a sortie of Genera sinking an entire Quarg patrol before the Quarg's intrasystem scanners even had a lock on them.

name: Supratribus
description: The Supratribus is a focus-built artillery ship, designed to carry two gargantuan gauss cannons into battle, piercing through any amount of enemy ranks. By default, the two main cannons are the ship's only offensive weapon, so it requires support to be used effectively. As such, the Supratribus is only found among larger Navarch fleets.

name: Familia
description: A relic of an earlier age, the Familia-class missile cruiser has largely been supplanted by its smaller brother, the Subfamilia, in service. However, the Familia is still a long-ranged powerhouse, with its huge battery of Ransacker launchers and a pair of 7-62 batteries to shore up against more pinpoint targets. Additionally, the shield matrix is especially robust, adding noticeable amounts of durability (and weight) to an already difficult to hunt ship.

name: Ordo
description: The Ordo is a middleweight cruiser fielded by the Navarchs, designed to provide an effective anti-fighter platform while still retaining some long-range, anti-capital capability as well. To this end, it has only 3 main guns of reduced size, providing it with the ability to provide long-ranged support between fending off fighter sorties.

name: Infracohors
description: The Infracohors is a prototype "mini-battleship" designed as a testbed for the Navarchs' Shock Blaster turrets. The principle behind the design is that, by removing a need for barbettes, a ship can carry more armor and equipment in a similar space. The resultant ship is thus more mobile, tougher, and still capable of long-ranged attacks.

name: Divisio
description: The Divisio is a heavy battleship employed by the Navarchs, intended to be a capital ship counterpart to the Regnum. Armed more heavily than any human ship could dream of being, it Divisio is designed for both anti-capital and anti-fighter roles, with heavy anti-missile defenses to ensure it remains effective in the face of long-range bombardments.

name: Phylum
description: The Phylum is an experimental battleship featuring a number of new and exciting technologies by the Navarchs; most notably, their new beam weapon, the THEL. Armed for heavy anti-capital duty, the Phylum is a fearsome ship to engage, however a reduced amount of deck space compared to the tried and true Divisio means that the ships tend to fare well in different conditions, rather than one being overwhelmingly favourable compared to the other.

name: Regalis
description: The Regalis Super Battleship is a sight to behold. Featuring 6 barbettes carrying some of the largest turrets the Navarchs have ever designed, it is capable of annihilating most ships with a single blast from its main battery. However, the ship is also coated in anti-missile and anti-fighter turrets, with powerful Phase Torpedoes to strike distant foes. Perhaps expectedly, the ship's only major weakness is its speed.

name: Infravitae
description: Though its range for a Navarch capital ship is slightly piteous, the Infravitae is a monster of a vessel, capable of melting apart hulls with its array of twelve Tactical High-Energy Laser nodes. This, combined with improved engine systems and the upgraded hull all experimental Navarch vessels share, results in a beast of a warship, capable of blasting almost any other vessel out of the sky.

name: Klados
description: The Klados' design harkens back to the early days of the Quarg-Navarch war, when the Navarchs were looking for the fastest ways to amplify their fighting force. The Klados is a modular warship whose design, unfortunately, never got off the ground. However, the Navarch industrial sector is now far more robust, and some rekindled interest has led to an "explorative" reintroduction of the design. 	In its base configuration, the Klados stands out as one of the only Navarch light warships designed to "brawl" with other ships at short to medium range, rather than relying on missiles or other artillery-esque features to vex the foe from great range.

name: Klados I
description: This is the first of three "iterative configurations" on the Klados. This first model retains a good portion of its mobility while delivering far more bang to the battlefield, with twice the amount of capital turrets.

name: Klados II
description: The second iteration on the Klados features the most balanced performance, barely scraping into the heavy warship size bracket while remaining relatively light and mobile. Its range, however, leaves something to be desired.

name: Klados III
description: This is the first of three "iterative configurations" on the Klados. This first model retains a good portion of its mobility while delivering far more bang to the battlefield, with twice the amount of capital turrets.

name: Nixus
description: The Nixus serves the role of a lightweight refuelling ship in Navarch fleets, capable of swiftly darting around a fleet to refuel ships in need while also providing a consistent source of fuel for long journeys. The ship also wields a pair of Phase Torpedo tubes, allowing it to pelt enemies from a distance during combat.

name: Subclassis
description: The Subclassis is designed as an all-in-one auxiliary ship for Navarch military fleets; it carries a large amount of passengers, cargo, and fuel as part of standard operations, though its armament leaves much to be desired compared to similarly sized warships like the Ordo.

name: Microphylum
description: Navarch society's vision of luxury rarely matches up with that of humanity's; particularly in the way that a "lavish" lifestyle in Navarch society is often described as serving aboard a large, powerful vessel, rather than spending most of their golden years on a rich planet surrounded by other rich folks. However, there is a subset of Navarch culture that doesn't mind a bit of extra comfort, and many of them end up travelling aboard ships like the Microphylum. 	While the conditions aboard the ship aren't "luxurious" by most species' standards, the beds are at least big enough for any Navarch of reasonable size to comfortably sleep in, and there's more interesting food aboard than the taffylike rations Navarch crew often subsist off of.

name: Imperium
description: The Imperium-class Auxiliary makes the Republic Navy's own auxiliary look pitiful in comparison; carrying a formidable main battery in its own right with nearly triple the capacity of its smaller sibling on all fronts, the Imperium is a staple of large Navarch fleets.

name: Dominium
description: The Dominium is a large-scale freighter that entered Navarch space quite recently, dating back about half a millenium. Over the course of its short life, it has seen massive popularity in the Navarchs' shipping sectors, able to carry an enormous amount of cargo wherever its needed. It is quite the sluggish ship, but Navarch captains are no stranger to bulky, slow ships.

name: Quarg Battleproteus
description: These are light, blindingly fast automatons used by the Quarg. On death, they overload their antimatter core, causing an uncharacteristically massive explosion.
